Problem Definition.definition: Identifying and clearly describing the real-world problem, classifying its type, and formulating objectives, constraints, and functions for computational modelling.

Data Preparation.definition: Collecting, cleaning, transforming, and organizing datasets, including feature engineering, to make them ready for model use.

Model Development and Training.definition: Implementing chosen algorithms in code, applying optimization techniques, and training models on prepared data.

Model Evaluation and Refinement.definition: Assessing model performance using appropriate metrics, tuning parameters, comparing approaches, and improving results.

Analysis and Reporting.definition: Interpreting results, explaining trade-offs and insights, and presenting findings in a clear, stakeholder-friendly manner.
